  1. Krum, the Library

Krum, the Library¶

A research framework for Byzantine-resilient distributed learning.

Krum provides a modular environment for comparing robust aggregation rules, simulating Byzantine attacks, running experiment campaigns, and analyzing results. The project is intentionally lightweight: clone the repository, add a Python file to the right folder, and the new component is immediately available by name.

Documentation¶

Type

Purpose

Tutorials

Step-by-step lessons for beginners.

How-to guides

Task-oriented recipes for common operations.

Reference

Technical descriptions of modules, classes, and functions.

Explanation

Background, design rationale, and conceptual discussion.

The Five-Step Workflow¶

A typical Krum experiment follows a simple pipeline:

  1. Build a model, dataset, loss, criterion, and optimizer.

  2. Train honestly — each step produces gradients from good workers.

  3. Attack — Byzantine workers submit malicious gradients.

  4. Aggregate — a robust rule combines honest and Byzantine gradients.

  5. Measure — results are logged, saved, and compared.

Three Layers¶

Layer

Purpose

Entry Point

Research Components

Robust aggregation rules, Byzantine attacks, model/dataset wrappers

Aggregators, Attacks, Experiments

Infrastructure

Contextual logging, argument parsing, tensor helpers, job management

Tools

Native Acceleration

Auto-compiled C++/CUDA extensions for hot paths

Native Acceleration

Project

  • Contributors

License¶

Krum is open-sourced under the MIT License. See the License file on GitHub for details. Source code and issue tracker live at https://github.com/calicarpa/krum. We welcome contributions and feedback from the community.
